expectations.text: Contains a concrete analogy using familiar real-world concepts
expectations.evidence: Twenty friends keeping identical copies of a notebook with permanent ink, writing transactions, verifying with the room.

expectations.text: Explicitly states where the analogy breaks down
expectations.evidence: Dedicated 'Where this analogy breaks down' paragraph about proof of work/stake vs. room vote.

expectations.text: Includes a visual (tldraw, Mermaid, or structured)
expectations.evidence: Mermaid flowchart included inline plus tldraw code saved to tldraw_code.js.

expectations.text: The visual teaches something the text alone does not
expectations.evidence: Visual shows three distinct views: transaction flow, block chain linkage, and 'why it matters' properties. The chain of blocks with hash references is structural info that the text describes but the diagram shows spatially.
Clear progression: One-Liner → Analogy → Visual → 'A Bit More Detail' (block/chain/distributed) → Why People Care → Go Deeper.

expectations.text: Includes curated external links with descriptions
expectations.evidence: Three links: 3Blue1Brown video, Anders Brownworth interactive demo, and companion walkthrough video, each with a sentence explaining value.

expectations.text: Technical terms introduced only after being tied to familiar concept
expectations.evidence: 'hash' introduced as 'a mathematical fingerprint', 'consensus' as 'agreement', 'nodes' as 'computers'. Each term follows the analogy version first.

expectations.text: No single section introduces more than 4 new concepts
expectations.evidence: Detail section is broken into three focused sub-sections (block, chain, distributed), each introducing 1-2 concepts.
